comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mtrmac* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [mtrmac] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@Luap99 @edsantiago Here I see tests failing on
> [FAIL] Podman run [It] [podman run and decrypt from local registry](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5809045066481664/html/int-podman-fedora-39-root-container-boltdb.log.html#t--Podman-run-podman-run-and-decrypt-from-local-registry--1)
>            /var/tmp/go/src/github.com[/containers/podman/test/e2e/run_test.go:2187](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/ab79c8e7869f6ba2c9b35af813f52d9e2fd92798/test/e2e/run_test.go#L2187)
>            [FAIL] Podman pull podman pull and decrypt [It] [From oci](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5809045066481664/html/int-podman-fedora-39-root-container-boltdb.log.html#t--Podman-pull-podman-pull-and-decrypt-From-oci--1)
>            /var/tmp/go/src/github.com[/containers/podman/test/e2e/pull_test.go:618](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/ab79c8e7869f6ba2c9b35af813f52d9e2fd92798/test/e2e/pull_test.go#L618)

where an image is pushed encrypted, removed locally, and then we test that a pull without an encryption key fails.

It _is_ the kind of thing that might well be broken by the work I am testing, but I don’t see this failing when testing locally, and I can’t currently see a code path that could cause an unexpected reuse.

Is it possible that this is newly being triggered by a race in tests? I’m afraid I’m only vaguely aware of the test changes in Podman.

> Is it possible that this is newly being triggered by a race in tests?

Trying now with more unique image names, which _might_ explain this.

Like you, I'm unable to reproduce locally. Even with `CI_USE_REGISTRY_CACHE=1` (which was a long shot anyway). 

For reference, this is the (correct) output from a recent run on another PR:
```
           Trying to pull localhost:5006/my-alpine:latest...
           Getting image source signatures
           Copying blob sha256:da4dd051a6bdde5e43f55eafc0cd715657f54b80f88af6742a53971eb71e4a39
           Error: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:da4dd051a6bdde5e43f55eafc0cd715657f54b80f88af6742a53971eb71e4a39": unpacking failed (error: exit status 1; output: archive/tar: invalid tar header)
           exhausting input failed (error: archive/tar: invalid tar header)
```
(that is also what I see on my f40 laptop).

And this is what we see in CI for this PR:
```
           Trying to pull localhost:5006/my-alpine-podman-run-and-decrypt:latest...
           Getting image source signatures
           Copying blob sha256:937f71f790005574ab9b667290dbfe40df78b0b200fa42631e755f212ddd73be
           Copying config sha256:4819930274ea2bd6ab611972f9928e07b84edcbfe1d2462fb2941957308dfae2
           Writing manifest to image destination

           [FAILED] Failure recorded during attempt 1:
           Command exited with status 0 (expected 125)
```
I'm confused too.

I’m sorry, this _is_ actually directly related to the c/image changes I’m testing. It does not reproduce in isolation, the failure happens because the BlobInfoCache has a record of a previous, unrelated, push of the same image.
I have split the Podman-side test fixes into #23379; this now tests the combination of #23379 with the c/image PR.
